back_translation: A Psalm of Asaph. Surely God is good to Israel, to those who are pure in heart.
But as for me, my feet had almost stumbled; my steps had nearly slipped.
For I envied the arrogant when I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
They have no struggle in their death, and their bodies are healthy and well-fed.
They do not bear the burdens others bear, and they are not afflicted like ordinary people.
Therefore pride is like a necklace around them, and violence covers them like a garment.
Wickedness comes out of their wealth, and the imaginations of their hearts run wild.
They mock and speak with malice; with arrogance they threaten to oppress others.
They set their mouths against the heavens, and their tongues range over the earth.
So their people turn to this place and drink water to the full.
The wicked say, 'How can God know? Does the Most High have knowledge?'
Behold, this is what the wicked are like—always at ease, and their wealth increases.
Surely in vain I have kept my heart pure and washed my hands in innocence.
For I am afflicted all day long and punished every morning.
If I had said, 'I will speak this way,' I would have betrayed Your children.
When I tried to understand all this, it was troublesome in my sight,
until I entered the sanctuary of God; then I understood their end.
Surely You set them on slippery ground; You cast them down into ruin.
In an instant they are destroyed, completely swept away by terrors!
O Lord, like one waking from a dream, when You arise You will despise their phantom image.
When my heart was bitter and my inmost being was pierced,
I was foolish and without knowledge; I was like a beast before You.
Yet I am always with You; You hold my right hand.
You guide me with Your counsel, and afterward You will receive me into glory.
Whom do I have in heaven but You? And having You, I desire no one on earth besides You.
Though my flesh and my heart may fail, God is the rock of my heart and my portion forever.
For behold, those who are far from You will perish; You destroy all who are unfaithful to You.
But as for me, it is good to draw near to God; I have made the Lord GOD my refuge, that I may tell of all Your works.
